title: Development has moved to github-stats-extended.
author: martin-mfg
body:
Hi everyone,

maintenance activity in this project has been limited for some time. To keep the project usable and to add new features, active development has moved to [github-stats-extended](https://github.com/stats-organization/github-stats-extended). This extended version of github-readme-stats is being maintained by @marcalexiei and me, with help from the community and from @rickstaa, one of the maintainers of github-readme-stats.

We want to keep this great project alive, but since the current maintainers in the github-readme-stats repository have only limited access rights, adding contributors here and keeping the original Vercel deployment stable has become impossible.

**We encourage everyone to switch to [github-stats-extended](https://github.com/stats-organization/github-stats-extended) for new features, fixes and a stable Vercel deployment.**

To quickly migrate to github-stats-extended, see [here](https://github.com/stats-organization/github-stats-extended#migration-from-github-readme-stats). For a list of new features in github-stats-extended see [here](https://github.com/stats-organization/github-stats-extended/blob/master/docs/fork.md). We also maintain [github-readme-stats-action](https://github.com/stats-organization/github-readme-stats-action), a GitHub Action which creates your stats cards in a GitHub workflow instead of fetching them from a Vercel server.

Finally, we want to thank @anuraghazra and everyone else who worked on github-readme-stats over the years!

title: Potential license attribution issue in derivative repository
author: Linvin-1233
body:
Hi, thanks for maintaining this project — it's widely used and very well designed.

While exploring GitHub stats implementations, I noticed a repository that appears to be 
substantially derived from this project:

https://github.com/ishandutta2007/beautiful-github-homepage

**Observations:**

1. **Core Algorithm** - `calculateRank.js` appears identical, including:
   - Same mathematical formulas (exponential_cdf, log_normal_cdf)
   - Identical weight coefficients
   - Same rank level system

2. **Type Definitions** - TypeScript interfaces in `src/cards/types.d.ts` 
   and `src/fetchers/types.d.ts` match exactly

3. **Project Architecture** - Largely mirrors this repository:
   - Similar directory structure (src/cards, src/fetchers, src/common)
   - Renamed but functionally equivalent files (e.g., repo.js → repo-card.js)
   - Identical theme configurations

4. **License Concern** ⚠️
   - Original repo has: `Copyright (c) 2020 Anurag Hazra`
   - Other repo has: `Copyright (c) 2020 Ishan`
   - MIT license requires attribution preservation

**The question:**

Rather than assuming intent, I wanted to ask: Are you aware of this repository? 
And if so, is the copyright notice removal something you're concerned about from a 
licensing perspective?

If this is indeed derived work, would you want the original attribution 
restored to comply with MIT license requirements?

Thanks for your consideration.

title: Repository Pin/Gist Card Description Overflow
author: hyperfinitism
body:
### Describe the bug

The repo/gist pin card's description text may overflow because:

1. Server-side line wrapping is character-count-based — `wrapTextMultiline()` in `src/common/fmt.js` wraps at 59 characters using the wrap library
2. The result is rendered as static SVG `<text>` + `<tspan>` — no browser-side reflow
3. Font fallback varies by OS — the CSS specifies `'Segoe UI', Ubuntu, Sans-Serif`, which resolves to:
   - Windows: Segoe UI
   - Linux: Ubuntu
   - macOS Chrome: Helvetica Neue (wider glyphs than the other fonts)
   - iOS Safari: San Francisco
4. The 59-character assumption does not fit Helvetica Neue, causing overflow on macOS frequently
5. If there are many wide characters (such as `O`), overflow is likely to occur. Conversely, if there are too many narrow characters (such as `i`), the actual text width per line becomes smaller, which doesn’t look good.

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Screenshots / Live demo link

_No response_

### Additional context

### Root cause

`wrapTextMultiline()` wrapped text by character count (59 chars), not by the actual line width.

### Proposed fix

Pixel-width-based word wrapping using the existing `measureText()` function and change line-break positions according to the font.

### Related PR

#4863 (introducing pixed-width-based word wrapping)

title: Option to count all "Contributed to"
author: rtritto
body: ### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe. "Contributed to (last year)" counts only contributions related to the last year. ### Describe the solution you'd like Add an option to count all contributions (from start including all years). E.g.: `&include_all_contributed=true` With the option enabled: - the label will be "Contributed to" - the stats of all contributions will be calculated in the rank ### Describe alternatives you've considered _No response_ ### Additional context _No response_

title: Dependency alert: axios compromised versions may be resolved via ^1.13.1
author: hyperfinitism
body:
There is an ongoing public supply-chain compromise affecting `axios@1.14.1` and `axios@0.30.4` on npm.

https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/10604

This repository currently declares:

```json
"axios": "^1.13.1"
```

So even if the current lockfile is still safe, future lockfile refreshes / fresh installs that re-resolve dependencies may pick up a compromised Axios release.

Would you consider urgently:

1. pinning Axios to a known-good version,
2. checking that neither `axios@1.14.1` / `0.30.4` nor `plain-crypto-js` appears in the dependency tree? 

This seems worth addressing quickly because many users self-host or fork this project and may run fresh installs.

title: Missing license attribution for Primer Octicons SVG paths in `src/common/icons.js
author: hyperfinitism
body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

The SVG path data in [icons.js](https://github.com/anuraghazra/github-readme-stats/blob/5df91f9bfa89c356a55cbb3c2bbc164fdbf94a86/src/common/icons.js#L3-L17) appears to be sourced from [Primer Octicons](https://github.com/primer/octicons). I could not find a corresponding copyright notice or license attribution in the repository. Primer Octicons is distributed under the [MIT License](https://github.com/primer/octicons/blob/main/LICENSE), which requires:

> The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all copies or substantial portions of the Software.

### Describe the solution you'd like

Either:

- Adding a `NOTICE` file with the Octicons copyright notice and Octicons `LICENSE.txt`
- Adding the Octicons copyright notice and license text to the repository `LICENSE` file

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_
